sentences.text: John and I got a big house for the summer.
A big house is very romantic.
This place is strange.
Why is the rent so cheap?
Why was it empty for so long?
John often laughs at me.
John is very practical.
John doesn't believe in things he can't see.
John is a doctor and I'm not getting better.
He thinks I'm not sick.
What can I do?
If a good doctor says you're not sick, what can you do?
My brother is also a doctor and agrees with John.
I take medicine and do things to get better.
I don't agree with them.
I think work would be good for me.
But what can I do?
I wrote for a bit but it made me tired.
I wish I could talk to more people but John says no.
So I'll talk about the house.
This place is very beautiful.
It's far from the road and the village.
It looks like English places with walls and many small houses.
The garden is very nice!
The garden is big and has many paths.
There were greenhouses, but they are broken now.
The big house has been empty for many years.
I feel something strange about the house.
John said I was feeling a draft and closed the window.
Sometimes I get very angry with John.
I was not this worried before.
I think I am worried because I am sad.
John says I should control myself more.
I don't like our room.
I wanted a room with roses and old curtains.
But John did not agree.
John said there was not enough space for two beds.
John is very careful and gives me many instructions.
I have a plan for each hour and John takes care of me.
John said we came here for me to rest and get fresh air.
We can always breathe fresh air.
We took the top room of the house.
The room is big with many windows.
The room was a nursery then a playground.
There are rings on the walls.
The yellow paper looks like boys used it.
The yellow paper is off the wall near my bed.
I have never seen worse yellow paper.
The pattern on the yellow paper is bad.
The pattern is hard to follow and makes you study it.
The color is a bad yellow.
The color is orange in some places.
The children did not like it.
I would not like to live in this room.
John is coming and he does not like me writing.
I have not felt like writing for two weeks.
I am writing by the window in the room.
John is away all day and sometimes at night.
I am happy my case is not bad.
I am very worried and sad.
It's just worry.
I feel bad not doing my job!
I wanted to help John but I am a problem.
It's hard for me to do small things like dressing and managing things.
Mary is good with the baby.
The baby is so sweet!
I can't be with him, it makes me worried.
John has never been worried.
He laughs at me about this yellow paper!
He wanted to change the yellow paper but then said it was bad for me.
He said if we change the yellow paper, I will worry about other things.
He said this house is helping me and he doesn't want to change it.
I said let's move downstairs, the rooms are nice.
He hugged me and said we can even go to the basement.
He is right about the bed and windows.
The room is big and nice, I don't want to make him sad.
I like the big room but not the yellow paper.
I can see the garden and trees from one window.
From another window, I can see the sea and a small dock.
I think I see people walking but John told me to control my thoughts.
I try sometimes.
I wish I could write to feel better.
I get tired when I try.
I feel lost without help in my work.
John says we will invite Henry and Julia when I am better.
I want to get better quickly.
I should not think about that.
The yellow paper looks bad to me.
There is a spot on the paper that looks like a face.
I am mad at the paper.
The eyes on the paper are everywhere.
There is a place where the eyes on the paper are not aligned.
I have never seen a thing show so much.
As a kid, I found walls and furniture scary and fun.
I remember friendly furniture in our old house.
I felt safe in one chair in our old house.
The furniture in this room is from downstairs.
This room was a playroom before.
The kids made a big mess in this room.
The yellow paper is torn and hard to remove.
The floor is scratched and the big bed looks like it was in a war.
I don't mind anything but the yellow paper.
John's sister is coming.
She is a nice girl and takes good care of me.
I must not let her see me write.
She loves cleaning the house and likes her job.
She thinks writing made me sick.
I can write when she is not here and watch her from the window.
There is a window that looks at the road and one that looks at the field.
The field is beautiful with big trees and grass.
The yellow paper has a pattern that you can only see in some light.
Where the sun hits the paper, I can see a strange shape.
His sister is on the stairs.
The Fourth of July is over.
The guests have gone and I am tired.
John thought it would be good for me to see my family.
I did not do any work.
Jennie does all the work now.
But I am still tired.
John says if I don't get better, he will send me to the doctor.
I don't want to go there.
I had a friend who saw the same doctor as me.
It's a long trip.
I don't want to do anything and I feel very bad.
I cry a lot.
I don't cry when John or others are here, only when I'm alone.
I am alone a lot now.
John is busy and Jennie leaves me alone when I want.
I walk in the garden, sit on the porch and spend time in my room.
I like the room, even with the yellow paper.
Maybe I like it because of the yellow paper.
I think about it all the time!
I lie in bed and look at the pattern for hours.
It's like a workout.
I start at the bottom and try to follow the pattern.
I know about design and this yellow paper doesn't follow any rules.
The pattern repeats sideways.
Each line looks different.
They connect at an angle and look like running seaweed.
The design stretches sideways and it's hard to understand.
They used a strip as a border, adding to the confusion.
In one part of the room, the yellow paper is almost whole.
When the light fades and the sun shines on it, I see shapes.
It's hard to follow it.
I think I will sleep.
I don't know why I'm writing this.
I don't want to.
I don't feel like it.
John would think it's silly.
I need to say how I feel and think.
But it's getting harder to do.
I'm often tired and I lie down a lot.
John wants me to stay strong and gives me fish oil and pills.
Dear John!
He loves me and doesn't like to see me sick.
I tried to talk to him about visiting Henry and Julia.
But he said I wasn't well enough to go.
It's hard for me to think clearly.
I guess I'm just worried and sad.
John took care of me and read to me until I was tired.
Only I can help myself.
The baby is happy and not in the room with the yellow paper.
The child would have used it if we hadn't.
I am lucky to escape!
I don't want my child to live in that room.
I am glad John kept me here.
I can handle it better than a baby.
I don't talk about it but I watch it.
Only I know what's in the yellow paper.
The shapes in the pattern become clearer every day.
The shape is always the same.
It looks like a woman hiding behind the pattern.
I don't like it.
I wish John would take me away.
It's hard to talk to John about how I feel.
I tried it last night.
It was a night with moonlight.
The moon shines like the sun.
I don't like seeing it move slowly through the window.
John was sleeping so I watched the moonlight on the yellow paper.
A person behind seemed to shake the yellow paper, wanting to get out.
I got up quietly to see if the paper moved, and John woke up.
He asked, 'What is it, little girl?'
He told me not to walk around or I'll get cold.
He said we can't leave because our lease isn't up.
He can't leave town because our big house is being fixed.
He said he would take me away if I was in danger.
He said he's a doctor and he knows I'm better.
He said I'm eating more and looking better.
He said he feels more relaxed about me.
I said I don't weigh more and I eat less when he's not here.
He said I can be as sick as I want.
He said we should sleep and talk in the morning.
I asked him not to leave.
He said he can't leave and we'll go on a trip in three weeks.
He said I'm better and looked at me sternly when I disagreed.
He asked me not to think I'm not better.
He said it's a bad thought.
He asked me to trust him as a doctor.
I stayed awake for hours thinking about the yellow paper.
The color is ugly and the pattern is very bad.
You think you know it, but then it changes.
It feels like a hard hit in the face.
It feels like a bad dream.
The outside pattern looks like a fungus.
Think of mushrooms growing in many ways - that's how it is.
Sometimes!
The yellow paper changes with the light.
When the first sun ray comes in, it changes fast.
That's why I always look at it.
In moonlight, it looks different.
At night, it becomes bars in any light.
I mean the outside pattern, and the woman behind it is clear.
It took time to see a woman in the pattern.
During the day, she is calm.
I think the pattern makes her still.
It's hard to understand.
It keeps me quiet for a long time.
I lie down a lot now.
John says sleep is good for me.
He made me rest for an hour after each meal.
I think it's a bad habit because I don't sleep.
I don't tell them I'm awake.
I'm starting to be scared of John.
John and Jennie act weird sometimes.
Sometimes I think it's the yellow paper.
I saw John looking at the yellow paper.
Jennie too.
I saw Jennie touch the yellow paper.
She got mad when I asked her about the yellow paper.
She said the yellow paper makes everything yellow.
She sounded nice.
But I know she was looking at the yellow paper.
Life is more fun now.
I have more things to look forward to.
I eat better and I'm less worried.
John is happy to see me get better.
John said I'm doing well despite the yellow paper.
I laughed it off.
I didn't tell him it was because of the yellow paper.
He may want to take me away.
I don't want to leave until I know.
One more week will be enough.
I feel much better!
I don't sleep at night but sleep a lot in the day.
It's hard and confusing in the day.
There are always new things on the fungus.
I can't keep track of them.
The yellow paper is strange!
It reminds me of bad yellow things.
The paper has a smell!
I noticed the smell when we came in.
The smell is here after a week of fog and rain.
The smell is all over the house.
I find the smell in many rooms.
The smell gets in my hair.
I can smell it even when I go for a ride.
The smell is very special!
I've tried to know what it smells like.
The smell is not bad but it stays.
This wet weather is bad.
I wake up and see it over me.
It used to bother me.
I thought about burning the house to remove the smell.
But now I am used to it.
It looks like the color of the paper!
A yellow smell.
There's a weird mark on this wall, near the bottom.
A line that goes around the room.
It goes behind all the furniture, except the bed.
I wonder how it was done and why.
Going round and round makes me feel dizzy!
I have found something at last.
I've figured it out by watching a lot at night.
The front pattern moves.
The woman behind shakes it!
Sometimes I think there are many women behind.
In bright spots she stays still, in dark spots she shakes the bars.
She always tries to climb through.
But no one can climb through that pattern.
They get in, then the yellow paper makes them worried and turns them white.
If those heads were not there, it would be better.
I think that woman goes out in the day!
I have seen her, I'll tell you why.
I can see her from all my windows!
It's the same woman, she's always moving slowly in the day.
I see her moving slowly up and down the long road.
I see her moving around the garden.
I see her on the road, hiding when a car comes.
I don't blame her.
It must be hard to be seen moving slowly in the day!
I always lock the door when I move slowly in the day.
I can't do it at night, John would know.
John is strange now, I don't want to make him sad.
I wish he would use another room!
I don't want anyone else to see that woman at night.
I wonder if I could see her from all the windows.
I can only see out of one window at a time.
Even though I always see her, she might move faster than me!
I've seen her moving fast in the open country, like a cloud shadow.
I wish I could take off the top pattern.
I will try it slowly.
I found something weird but won't say it now.
It's not good to trust people too much.
We have two days to take off the yellow paper and John may see.
I don't like his eyes.
He asked Jennie many things about me.
She gave a good report.
She said I sleep a lot in the day.
John knows I don't sleep well at night but I'm quiet.
He asked me many things and acted kind.
I can see through him!
I don't blame him for acting like this after sleeping under the yellow paper.
It interests me, but John and Jennie are affected by it.
Hurray!
This is the last day, but it's enough.
John is in town and will be back in the evening.
Jennie wanted to sleep with me.
I told her I would sleep better alone.
That was smart, because I wasn't alone.
When it was night, I ran to help her.
We took off a lot of yellow paper.
The paper was as high as my head.
I said I would finish it today when the sun came up.
They are moving my things back tomorrow.
Jennie was surprised, but I said I did it because I was angry.
She said she wanted to do it too, but I should not get tired.
She told on herself that time!
I'm here and no one touches the yellow paper but me.
She wanted me to leave the room.
I said it was quiet and clean now, so I would sleep.
Now she's gone, and the big bed is all that's left.
We'll sleep downstairs tonight and go home by boat tomorrow.
I like the room now that it's empty.
The children ran a lot here!
This bed is almost broken!
But I need to start working.
I've locked the door and thrown the key outside.
I don't want to leave or let anyone in until John comes.
I want to surprise John.
I have a rope here that Jennie didn't find.
If the woman tries to run, I can tie her up!
I forgot I can't reach far without something to stand on!
This bed doesn't move!
I tried to move the bed until I was tired.
Then I took off all the yellow paper I could reach.
The yellow paper sticks badly and the pattern likes it!
All those heads and eyes and growths laugh at me!
I'm getting so mad I might do something big.
Jumping out the window would be good exercise, but the bars are too strong.
But, I wouldn't do it.
Of course not.
I know that move is wrong and could be misunderstood.
I don't like to look out the windows—there are too many fast women.
I wonder if they all come from the yellow paper like me?
But now I'm tied up with my hidden rope—you won't find me on the road!
I guess I'll have to go back behind the pattern when it's night, and that's hard!
It's nice to be in this big room and move as I want!
I don't want to go outside.
I won't, even if Jennie asks me to.
You have to move low outside where it's green, not yellow.
I can move easily here and I know my way.
Look, John is at the door!
You can't open it, young man!
He shouts and hits a lot!
Now he wants an axe.
It's bad to break that nice door!
I told John the key is under a leaf by the steps.
Then he asked me to open the door.
I said I can't and the key is under a leaf by the door.
He found the key and came in.
He stopped at the door.
He asked what's happening.
I kept moving and looked back at him.
I said I got out and it's not because of you or Jane.
I took down the yellow paper so you can't lock me up.
